title: Verify existing JWT token validation in auth middleware
description: JWT token validation already exists in the auth middleware. The `src/middleware/auth-middleware.ts` uses `JwtService.verify(token)` to validate JWT tokens, and `src/auth/withAuth.ts` uses `AuthService.verifyAccessToken()` which also calls `JwtService.verify()` internally. No additional JWT validation needs to be added — the feature is implemented and verified.
existing_patterns: src/middleware/auth-middleware.ts: Factory pattern `createAuthMiddleware(userStore, sessionStore, jwtService)` with JWT verification via `jwtService.verify(token)`
src/auth/withAuth.ts: Next.js HOC pattern wrapping route handlers with `AuthService.verifyAccessToken(token)`
src/auth/jwt-service.ts: `JwtService` class with `sign()`, `verify()`, `signAccessToken()`, `signRefreshToken()` methods using HMAC-SHA256
src/auth/_auth.ts: `extractBearerToken()` utility and `checkRole()` for RBAC hierarchy
src/auth/auth-service.ts: `AuthService.verifyAccessToken()` combines JWT verification with Payload user lookup
